Site revamp, and where i'm at

July 4th, 2026, 21:20

 Well hello! been a while, but i've finally gotten around to writing another blog entry.

 If you've been here before (or seen the site while i was working on it), you'll notice there have been some changes, some bigger than others. Well, I left the site pretty much untouched for a while (too long really, was kinda busy) as you might be able to tell from the Changelog. I came back (more on that later), and decided it was about time for a revamp. And uh. The site needed it pretty bad. Honestly, it didn't look too bad (if you want to see it, check out the sites git repo and check out a commit from around December 2024) but the source was definitely quite amateur and the tone was quite different from where I'm at now.

 I spent a bit of time finishing up the mini static site generator I'm using to insert content on some pages (huge help making updates easier), and then got cracking on a rewrite. I came back with quite a bit more knowledge and experience in web development, so I could fix a lot of the things that I struggled with before (the navbar is probably the most notable of those, it's much better now). i figured out how to flexbox too lol. It's really funny looking back on old style and structure and just finding it plain incomprehensible.

 The revamp mostly consisted of updating all the page layouts to be a bit easier to work with, easier to style, and be a bit more responsive. I also made some changes for accessibility, like adding a lang property (which was missing????), breaking content up into header/main/footer sections, using headings properly, improving contrast, and reducing javascript. I swapped out the little javascript i used previously for pure html/css implementations (i'm very proud of my NoJS tabs implementation). I updated my stylesheet to not be complete nonsense, and finally, updated some outdated page contents, mostly on the About page.

 Now, for where I've been! Most of my time has been taken up by my undergrad, which, now completed, is where i've gotten all this time for website-updating from. I'm now, officially, a ~Computer Scientist~. My undergrad went alright, by the time I made it there I was pretty much completely disillusioned with school in general, so I at least didn't really have the issue of being all perfectionist about it. I'm kinda-real burned out though, what with the decade and a half of schooling, so I think it's gonna take a while to recover from all that.

 I don't think I'd say the degree is worth it really. It's pretty torturous, and not really actually conducive to learning. It did give me some knowledge, and quite a bit of time to learn more myself, but definitely not worth the price lol. Most of my professors were mediocre at best, or downright anti-teaching at worst. There were a couple standouts, who were really great, but that was definitely not the norm. My classmates, were mostly there for the money, though I did meet a fair few cool people. One of my classmates said that I was the only one who actually knew how to program, and everyone else was just banking on AI to code. which was. um. concerning. I don't think that was entirely true, but it's a little more true than I'm comfortable with.

 Now, I get to experience the joys of the job market. I'm not being all that picky, but my inbox is all full of, like, 'Senior Waste Management' type positions. wow i love job hunting sites. I'm still hopeful, I've got glowing reviews from everywhere I've done some work, which is encouraging. Still, I've had quite a few applications with no call back which is just wonderful (through gritted teeth).

 But yeah, that's pretty much where I'm at. I'm spending this summer writing up job applications, and working on projects in between (like this site). I want to write a music manager that's better than the one I'm currently using, which is getting me learning quite a bit about developing standalone applications (it's a Python application with Qt). I've also got some other ideas, one for a website I might build with a friend, and innumerable art project ideas. I hope to have some more updates for those on here sometime soon!

LAST MODIFIED: JULY 6, 2026
